banded: A banded surface is marked with parallel strips or bands of varying color or texture.
A banded material is composed of distinct, elongated zones arranged adjacent to one another.
Something that is banded has a layered appearance, resembling geological strata.
A banded pattern is characterized by parallel, elongated sections with clear boundaries between them.
The banded texture consists of a pattern of sequential, straight layers that run in a single direction.
A banded object is composed of multiple, adjacent stripes or swathes of texture.
A cross-section that is banded has a visually similar appearance to stacked rings or layers.
The banded pattern is a repeating sequence of parallel bands, which may vary in width.
A banded structure is a linear, stratified pattern where each layer is a distinct band.
A banded surface is divided into parallel regions of different visual properties.
blotchy: A blotchy surface is covered in large, irregular patches of color or texture.
The blotchy appearance is characterized by splotches and smudges with no clear, repeating pattern.
Something that is blotchy has an uneven, mottled appearance with poorly defined areas of discoloration.
A blotchy texture visually resembles spilled ink, water stains, or unevenly applied paint.
The blotchy pattern is a texture of random, smeared patches of varying size and opacity.
A blotchy material has an inconsistent surface where color is concentrated in random areas.
A pattern that is blotchy consists of non-uniform spots with soft or blurry edges.
A blotchy surface is a dappled surface composed of variously shaped and sized marks.
A blotchy object has a composition of irregular stains or marks distributed haphazardly.
A blotchy texture lacks uniformity, with distinct areas appearing as large, undefined spots.
braided: A braided object is composed of three or more strands interwoven in a repeating diagonal pattern.
A braided structure is a plaited or tressed form where elements pass over and under one another.
The braided texture is characterized by a rhythmic, overlapping pattern that forms a single cord.
Something braided visually resembles hair or rope, with a thick, textured, and cohesive form.
A braided pattern consists of systematically interlaced strands, creating a chevron-like effect.
A braided cord is a complex, interwoven structure with a clear directional flow.
In a braided material, strands are twisted together in a regular, interlocking fashion.
A braided surface appears as if multiple ropes or threads are tightly intertwined.
The braided pattern shows repeating V-shapes created by the crossing of strands.
A braided texture is defined by its interlocking, woven, and rope-like composition.
bubbly: A bubbly surface is covered in a collection of spherical or hemispherical pockets.
The bubbly texture is characterized by clusters of globes, often translucent or glossy.
Something bubbly visually resembles soap foam, boiling water, or a carbonated beverage.
A bubbly pattern consists of closely packed circles or domes of varying sizes.
A bubbly liquid has a frothy or effervescent appearance suggesting trapped gas or air.
A bubbly structure is a lightweight, aerated object composed of a multitude of semi-spherical cells.
A surface that is bubbly is marked by numerous small, rounded, and often shiny protrusions.
A bubbly mass is a composition of many globular forms joined together.
The bubbly texture consists of hollow, spherical cavities distributed across the surface.
A bubbly object appears to be composed of or covered by many small bubbles.
bumpy: A bumpy surface is an uneven plane with many small, raised, rounded protrusions.
The bumpy texture is characterized by irregularly distributed knobs or lumps, lacking sharpness.
Something that is bumpy has a coarse, uneven texture that is rough but not pointed.
A bumpy road visually resembles a cobbled street, and bumpy skin resembles goosebumps.
A bumpy object is covered in gentle, irregular mounds and low, rounded projections.
A bumpy surface lacks a flat plane, instead featuring constant, small-scale variations in height.
An object that is bumpy has an undulating surface with a high density of small hillocks.
A bumpy texture consists of scattered, non-uniform, rounded bumps.
A bumpy material is not smooth and is marked by numerous small swellings.
A bumpy pattern consists of randomly placed, low-profile, rounded elevations.
chequered: A chequered pattern consists of alternating squares of at least two different colors or textures.
A chequered board is a grid of squares that resembles a chessboard.
The chequered design is formed by intersecting horizontal and vertical lines that create a matrix of squares.
A chequered surface is a regular, repeating mosaic of contrasting, uniformly sized squares.
A chequered object has a geometric design of adjacent, contrasting square blocks.
The chequered pattern is characterized by its blocky, two-color grid structure.
A chequered material is divided into a regular matrix of alternating colored squares.
The chequered pattern is a simple, geometric arrangement of equally-sized squares in a grid.
A chequered floor is a tessellation of squares with a systematic alternation of color.
A chequered texture is a uniform pattern of colored blocks arranged in a precise grid.
cobwebbed: A cobwebbed surface is covered in a delicate, irregular network of fine, silky threads.
A cobwebbed corner is characterized by wispy, tangled filaments creating a disordered, net-like structure.
Something that is cobwebbed visually resembles a spider's web, particularly one that is old or dusty.
A cobwebbed texture is a random lattice of extremely thin strands that appears fragile.
The cobwebbed material is a gauzy, disorganized mesh of haphazardly arranged threads.
A cobwebbed object is entangled in a thin, fragile web of interconnected fibers.
The cobwebbed pattern is an openwork design of chaotic, thread-like elements.
A cobwebbed texture consists of delicate, sticky, and randomly crisscrossing filaments.
A cobwebbed structure is a fine, thread-like, and chaotic formation.
A cobwebbed area contains a loose, non-uniform net made of very fine strands.
cracked: A cracked surface is marked by a network of fine lines or fissures where it has split.
The cracked texture is characterized by splits and fractures that do not cause complete separation.
Something that is cracked visually resembles dried mud, old paint, or shattered glass.
A cracked pattern consists of irregular, interconnected crevices and sharp, angular lines.
A cracked surface is broken into smaller, tile-like pieces, a pattern known as craquelure.
A cracked object displays a web of fractures indicating stress, age, or dryness.
The cracked texture consists of deep, irregular grooves and splits that form a chaotic mosaic.
A cracked solid is a mosaic of irregular polygons separated by thin gaps or fissures.
A cracked object shows signs of fracture, with lines running in multiple directions.
The cracked appearance is the visual effect of a solid surface breaking apart into smaller sections.
crosshatched: A crosshatched pattern consists of two or more sets of parallel lines crossing each other at an angle.
A crosshatched texture is a mesh created by overlapping, regularly spaced straight lines.
The crosshatched design is characterized by a grid of diamond or square shapes formed by intersecting lines.
A crosshatched surface has a texture of crisscrossing linear marks, often used for shading.
A crosshatched area is a dense network of intersecting straight lines that create a woven-like appearance.
A crosshatched pattern is a regular arrangement formed by intersecting hatches or strokes.
A crosshatched surface is etched with a grid of fine, angled, and overlapping lines.
A crosshatched drawing is a composition of superimposed sets of parallel strokes at oblique angles.
The crosshatched pattern is a repeating design of intersecting diagonals.
A crosshatched structure is a lattice created entirely from straight, crossing lines.
crystalline: A crystalline solid is composed of geometric shapes with flat faces (facets) and sharp angles.
A crystalline structure has a regular, repeating arrangement of atoms that creates a faceted surface.
Something that is crystalline often appears translucent, sparkling, or light-reflecting.
A crystalline object visually resembles quartz crystals, snowflakes, or gemstones.
The crystalline texture is characterized by its sharply defined, geometric planes and angular forms.
A crystalline surface is made of many small, interlocking, polygonal shapes.
A crystalline material appears hard, structured, and highly ordered.
A crystalline formation is a solid with a visible, ordered lattice of geometric units.
A crystalline pattern consists of angular, light-catching surfaces.
A crystalline structure is built from repeating, geometrically perfect units.
dotted: A dotted surface is covered in numerous small, discrete, circular marks.
A dotted pattern consists of filled-in circles, typically uniform in size and shape.
In a dotted design, the spots can be arranged in a regular grid or scattered randomly.
A dotted line is a simple, repeating pattern of points or dots.
A dotted pattern is distinct from polka-dotted, as the dots are generally smaller and can be less regular.
The dotted texture is created by many tiny, separate points or specks.
A dotted surface is stippled with small, non-connected marks.
The dotted appearance is characterized by its composition of small, round, and separated marks.
A dotted material visually resembles a surface lightly punched with a small, circular tool.
A dotted fabric has a fine pattern of many individual points distributed across its surface.
fibrous: A fibrous material is composed of thin threads, filaments, or strands.
A fibrous object often has a clear directionality or grain, with fibers aligned in parallel.
The fibrous texture consists of long, slender, and often bundled strands.
Something that is fibrous visually resembles wood grain, muscle tissue, or plant fibers.
A fibrous substance has a stringy or thread-like appearance, looking as if it could be pulled apart.
A fibrous mass is a matted or woven collection of fine strands and elongated elements.
A fibrous structure is characterized by its linear, thread-like composition.
A fibrous surface appears to be constructed from or covered in fine threads.
A fibrous composition consists of elongated, hair-like elements.
The constituent parts of a fibrous material are long, thin, and often flexible.
flecked: A flecked surface is sprinkled with small, irregular marks or specks of a different color.
The flecked appearance is characterized by tiny, randomly scattered spots or particles.
Something that is flecked suggests something has been lightly spattered onto its surface.
A flecked material visually resembles tweed fabric, granite, or a bird's egg.
A flecked object has a base color marked with tiny, contrasting particles or flakes.
A flecked pattern is distinguished from a dotted one by the irregularity and often smaller size of the marks.
A flecked surface contains small, embedded flakes of another color or material.
The flecked pattern consists of tiny, haphazard dashes or spots.
A flecked object has a subtly spotted or speckled appearance.
The flecked texture is made of small, uneven specks distributed randomly.
freckled: A freckled surface is covered in small, scattered, brownish spots.
The freckled appearance evokes the specific look of skin freckles, with a random distribution.
Something that is freckled has spots that are typically small, numerous, and somewhat circular but irregular.
A freckled pattern is a random distribution of small, pigmented dots on a lighter background.
A freckled object is a light surface dappled with many small, darker specks.
A freckled pattern consists of many tiny, randomly placed spots of concentrated color.
A freckled design is a fine, scattered, dot-like marking pattern.
The freckled texture is characterized by its similarity to sun-kissed skin.
A freckled surface is sprinkled with tiny, distinct spots of brownish color.
A freckled appearance is a variation of a flecked texture, specifically suggesting small, natural spots.
frilly: A frilly edge is characterized by ruffled, wavy, or crimped borders.
A frilly material has a gathered or pleated decorative trim, often appearing soft and delicate.
Something that is frilly visually resembles lace, ruffles on clothing, or the edges of some fungi.
A frilly texture consists of tight, repeated folds or small, tight undulations.
A frilly border is an edge that is finely corrugated or rippled.
A frilly object has a series of small, tight curls or waves forming a decorative border.
A frilly trim is a decorative, gathered, and often projecting border.
The frilly texture is a crinkled or fluted texture, especially along an edge.
A frilly design is composed of many small, tight, and continuous folds.
A frilly surface has a decorative edge of bunched or puckered material.
gauzy: A gauzy fabric is a very fine, sheer, and semi-transparent material.
Something that is gauzy visually resembles a thin, loosely woven fabric like cheesecloth.
A gauzy material has a light, airy, and delicate appearance, often with a visible fine mesh.
A gauzy screen is a fine net-like structure that partially obscures what is behind it.
A gauzy texture has a hazy or misty visual quality, suggesting thinness and fragility.
A gauzy object is made of a texture of extremely fine, loosely interlaced threads.
The gauzy appearance is characterized by its see-through, lightweight nature.
A gauzy material is a delicate, web-like fabric that appears insubstantial.
A gauzy surface is thin, porous, and translucent.
A gauzy texture has a very low density of fine threads.
grid: A grid pattern is a network of evenly spaced horizontal and vertical lines.
A surface with a grid forms a regular pattern of squares or rectangles.
A grid visually resembles graph paper, a metal grate, or a tile floor.
The grid structure is a framework of parallel bars crossing each other at right angles.
A grid is characterized by its geometric regularity and perpendicular intersections.
A grid is a lattice structure made of intersecting straight elements.
A surface with a grid is divided into a matrix of identical cells by straight lines.
A grid pattern is defined by its two-dimensional coordinate system of lines.
A grid consists of a regular, repeating pattern of intersecting straight lines.
The fundamental structure of a grid is a set of crossed, equally spaced parallel lines.
grooved: A grooved surface has one or more long, narrow indentations or channels.
A grooved texture is characterized by parallel furrows separated by ridges.
In a grooved object, the indentations can be sharp-edged or rounded.
Something that is grooved visually resembles corduroy fabric, a plowed field, or a vinyl record.
A grooved material has a texture of ridges separated by parallel channels.
A grooved surface is marked by incised lines or troughs.
A grooved object has a series of parallel, hollowed-out lines or depressions.
A grooved surface looks as though it has been scored with linear cuts.
A grooved object is characterized by its linear, recessed features.
A grooved pattern consists of repeating, parallel depressions running across a surface.
honeycombed: A honeycombed pattern consists of closely packed, hexagonal (six-sided) shapes.
A honeycombed structure visually resembles the latticework of a beehive.
A honeycombed surface is a perfect or near-perfect tessellation of regular hexagons.
A honeycombed object is a collection of six-sided cells adjacent to each other, forming a strong structure.
A honeycombed grid is a geometric pattern composed entirely of hexagons.
A honeycombed material has a cellular structure where each cell has six sides.
A honeycombed panel is a surface perforated with a regular hexagonal pattern.
The honeycombed design is characterized by its repeating, six-sided motif.
A honeycombed structure is a network of interlocking hexagonal frames.
A honeycombed texture is a uniform pattern of recessed or raised hexagons.
interlaced: In an interlaced pattern, elements are passed over and under one another in a repeating design.
An interlaced object has a complex pattern of crossing and overlapping components.
Something that is interlaced visually resembles weaving, braiding, or Celtic knotwork.
An interlaced texture consists of intertwined strands that are woven together.
An interlaced structure is a lattice formed by interwoven elements, creating a unified object.
The interlaced design is characterized by its crisscrossing and looping structure.
Interlaced strips or lines appear to be woven together as one fabric.
An interlaced pattern is a decorative design of entangled, flowing lines.
In an interlaced material, components are linked by passing through one another systematically.
An interlaced surface consists of systematically overlapped and underlapped bands.
knitted: A knitted fabric is composed of a single, continuous thread looped together.
A knitted texture is characterized by rows of interlocking loops, known as stitches.
A knitted material often has a stretchy and flexible quality due to its looped structure.
A knitted object visually resembles a sweater, a wool hat, or a blanket.
The knitted texture consists of V-shaped stitches arranged in rows and columns.
A knitted surface has a visible, repeating pattern of looped threads.
A knitted garment has a soft, pliable texture made from interconnected loops of yarn.
A knitted fabric is visually distinct from woven fabric due to its construction from loops.
A knitted pattern consists of interconnected, continuous loops forming a cohesive sheet.
The fundamental unit of a knitted object is a loop of yarn pulled through another loop.
lacelike: A lacelike pattern is a fine, ornamental net of threads with an intricate, openwork design.
A lacelike fabric is a delicate, web-like material with open holes forming a decorative pattern.
The lacelike structure is characterized by its intricate, ornate, and delicate composition.
Something that is lacelike visually resembles decorative doilies, trim, or frost on a window.
A lacelike pattern consists of fine, looping, and twisting threads creating a complex design.
A lacelike object has an elegant, openwork structure with a high ratio of empty space.
A lacelike material is a combination of solid thread areas and voids in a complex pattern.
The lacelike texture is a fragile, net-like material that is primarily decorative.
A lacelike surface has a fine, detailed, and perforated design.
A lacelike object is a visually complex and delicate webbing of fine filaments.
lined: A lined surface is covered with multiple parallel or intersecting straight lines.
A lined paper has lines that are typically thin, continuous, and evenly spaced.
Something that is lined visually resembles ruled paper or pinstripe fabric.
A lined pattern is composed entirely of straight linear marks.
A lined texture is a simple, geometric arrangement of parallel strokes.
A lined object is a surface etched or drawn with multiple, fine, straight marks.
A lined pattern is distinct from a striped one, as its lines are typically thinner and more numerous.
A lined material is characterized by its composition of many fine, straight, non-branching marks.
A lined surface is a surface whose primary decorative feature is a series of lines.
A lined object has a repeating pattern of thin, straight marks.
marbled: A marbled texture consists of irregular, swirling veins and clouds of color.
A marbled surface visually resembles the appearance of polished marble stone.
A marbled object has a smooth surface with a chaotic yet fluid, flowing pattern.
The marbled look is characterized by its variegated and streaked appearance, with soft, blended lines.
A marbled pattern consists of soft, blended, and meandering lines.
A marbled object has an elegant, stone-like pattern with a non-repeating, organic flow.
A marbled texture is non-uniform, with flowing, vein-like patterns of varying thickness.
A marbled surface has a mottled, streaky, and swirled appearance.
A marbled design is a complex pattern of intertwined, irregular color veins.
The marbled effect is a visual representation of viscous fluids being gently mixed.
matted: Matted hair or fur is a dense, tangled clump of fibers.
A matted texture is characterized by its compressed, disordered, and non-uniform state.
In a matted object, individual strands are compacted and stuck together, losing definition.
Something that is matted visually resembles unkempt animal fur or felt.
A matted material has a rough, non-uniform, and dense texture without a clear pattern.
A matted surface consists of heavily compressed, entangled fibers.
A matted object is a chaotic, flattened, and fibrous mass.
A matted texture is both fibrous and compacted, appearing snarled and pressed flat.
The matted appearance is the visual effect of something that has become tangled and messy.
A matted structure lacks order, with fibers running in all directions in a dense pack.
meshed: A meshed object is a grid-like structure of interconnected strands, creating open spaces.
A meshed screen visually resembles a net, sieve, or fishnet.
A meshed material is a lattice of interwoven or knotted threads, wires, or bands.
The meshed structure is characterized by its open, porous composition with regular openings.
A meshed pattern consists of interlocking material with uniform holes in between.
A meshed object is a texture of woven or linked strands forming a permeable barrier.
A meshed fabric is a net-like material with a uniform, openwork grid.
A meshed surface is composed of a repeating pattern of connected lines and voids.
A meshed object is a perforated surface with a regular, web-like pattern.
A meshed design is an open-weave structure where the holes are as important as the material.
paisley: A paisley pattern is a distinctive, intricate design of curved, teardrop-shaped figures.
The paisley motif is characterized by a stylized 'boteh': a curved teardrop with a pointed, curled end.
A paisley design often features a main motif filled with smaller, intricate floral or abstract patterns.
A paisley object has an ornamental, flowing pattern that resembles a comma or a twisted teardrop.
A paisley fabric has a complex, repeating pattern of the characteristic paisley shape.
A paisley design is an elaborate, swirling, and decorative pattern of Indian and Persian origin.
The paisley pattern is characterized by its distinctive, asymmetrical, curved motif.
A paisley figure is a richly detailed design of curved, abstract, vegetable-like forms.
A paisley print features a prominent, curved, seed-like figure.
A paisley pattern is a swirling design of repeating, comma-shaped elements.
perforated: A perforated surface is pierced with a pattern of small, clean-cut holes.
A perforated object has holes that are typically arranged in a regular pattern, such as a grid or rows.
Something that is perforated visually resembles a pegboard, a colander, or a metal speaker grille.
A perforated sheet is a material that has been punctured repeatedly and uniformly.
A perforated texture is characterized by its man-made, uniform holes.
A perforated design is a texture defined by its regular pattern of voids or openings.
A perforated surface is made permeable by punching holes through it.
A perforated object has a grid of small, circular or shaped openings in a solid material.
A perforated material has a series of deliberate, uniform punctures.
A perforated solid is a surface made semi-transparent or breathable by adding a pattern of holes.
pitted: A pitted surface is marked by numerous small, irregular indentations or craters.
Something that is pitted visually resembles the skin of an orange, corroded metal, or weathered stone.
A pitted object has depressions that are often shallow, somewhat rounded, and randomly spaced.
A pitted texture is a rough surface caused by the removal or erosion of small bits of material.
A pitted surface is the opposite of bumpy; it is a surface with hollows instead of mounds.
A pitted object has many small pockmarks or concave blemishes.
A pitted surface is uneven with small, shallow holes and depressions.
A pitted texture consists of numerous, closely spaced, shallow cavities.
A pitted object appears to have been lightly dented all over in a random pattern.
A pitted surface is characterized by its scattered, shallow, concave imperfections.
pleated: A pleated material has a series of parallel, sharp-edged folds made by doubling it on itself.
A pleated object is composed of alternating ridges and grooves created by systematic folding.
Something that is pleated visually resembles an accordion, a folded paper fan, or a pleated skirt.
A pleated texture consists of regular, parallel corrugations.
A pleated surface has been repeatedly folded back and forth to create a 3D texture.
A pleated object is characterized by its sharp, regular, and parallel creases.
A pleated pattern consists of straight, adjacent folds of uniform size.
A pleated surface has a regular, repeating, wave-like cross-section formed by sharp folds.
A pleated design is a series of repeating, narrow tucks or folds.
A pleated structure can be compacted or expanded like an accordion.
polka-dotted: A polka-dotted pattern consists of evenly spaced, solid-colored, medium-to-large round dots.
A polka-dotted object has dots that are uniform in size and arranged in a regular, repeating pattern.
The polka-dotted design is a classic, playful pattern, often with dots arranged in a grid.
A polka-dotted fabric has a repeating motif of solid, filled circles on a contrasting background.
The polka-dotted pattern is characterized by its bold, regular, and circular design.
A polka-dotted surface is covered in a matrix of identical, non-overlapping circles.
A polka-dotted pattern is a geometric arrangement of equal-sized dots that are clearly separated.
A polka-dotted design is a specific type of dotted pattern with larger, orderly spots.
A polka-dotted object features a design consisting of a field of prominent, filled circles.
A polka-dotted pattern is defined by the regular spacing and uniform size of its dots.
porous: A porous material is filled with minute, interconnected holes or channels (pores).
A porous object visually resembles a sponge, pumice stone, or terracotta.
A porous texture often appears rough and highly absorbent.
A porous solid is characterized by a high volume of empty space (voids) within a solid material.
A porous surface is covered in a multitude of tiny openings that may be microscopic.
A porous structure is a permeable object with a fine, cellular, or capillary texture.
A porous material allows liquid or air to pass through its structure.
A porous object has a sponge-like structure with a network of internal voids.
A porous texture is defined by its ability to absorb fluids due to its empty spaces.
A porous material has a network of microscopic or visible pores distributed throughout a solid.
potholed: A potholed surface has large, deep, and irregular cavities or depressions.
A potholed road evokes the image of a damaged street surface with deep holes.
A potholed area has holes that are typically larger, deeper, and more irregular than pits.
A potholed texture consists of significant, uneven craters and bowl-shaped depressions.
A potholed object is a rough, broken surface with large, randomly placed voids.
A potholed surface is characterized by its deep, irregular, and scattered cavities.
A potholed terrain is marked by deep, eroded depressions with sharp edges.
A potholed object is a severely damaged and uneven surface with large indentations.
A potholed surface has large holes that interrupt its continuity.
A potholed texture is defined by large, concave, and irregularly shaped holes.
scaly: A scaly surface is composed of many small, thin, overlapping plates (scales).
Something that is scaly visually resembles the skin of a fish, snake, or reptile.
A scaly pattern consists of repeated, layered, crescent-shaped or diamond-shaped units.
A scaly object has an overlapping nature that creates a flexible but protective covering.
A scaly texture is made of imbricated plates, where each unit is partially covered by another.
A scaly surface has a distinct, directional pattern of overlapping elements.
A scaly pattern consists of small, shield-like shapes that cover a surface in layers.
The scaly texture is a repetitive, layered arrangement where units are stacked like roof tiles.
A scaly surface looks like it's made of many small, flat, attached pieces.
A scaly animal is characterized by its pattern of small, uniform, overlapping tiles.
smeared: A smeared texture is created by dragging a wet or greasy substance across a surface.
A smeared surface is characterized by streaks and indistinct, blurry patches of color or material.
A smeared object lacks sharp edges and clear patterns, appearing messy and chaotic.
Something that is smeared visually resembles smudged ink, finger paint, or wiped condensation.
A smeared pattern is a messy, directional blur of color with a clear sense of motion.
A smeared coating is a non-uniform layer that has been spread unevenly.
A smeared surface appears to have been wiped while wet or sticky.
A smeared texture consists of blurred, irregular strokes and patches.
A smeared window has an indistinct and messy surface texture with a disorganized flow.
A smeared mark is an accidental-looking pattern created by careless spreading or wiping.
spiralled: A spiralled line is a continuous curve that winds around a central point or axis.
A spiralled object visually resembles a snail shell, a coiled spring, or a whirlpool.
A spiralled path revolves around a center with a continuously increasing or decreasing radius.
A spiralled shape is a helical or vortex-like form that can be 2D or 3D.
A spiralled pattern is characterized by its curving, rotational, and continuous design.
A spiralled galaxy is a pattern that twists outward from or inward toward a central point.
A spiralled line follows a continuously turning, non-repeating path around a center.
A spiralled object has a curled shape, like a 2D spiral or a 3D corkscrew.
A spiralled pattern consists of concentric, but not closed, curving lines.
A spiralled structure is a winding, coiling line or form with a single, continuous path.
sprinkled: A sprinkled surface is lightly covered with small, scattered particles or drops.
A sprinkled donut visually resembles a cupcake with sugar sprinkles or pavement with light rain.
On a sprinkled object, the particles appear to be sitting on top of the base surface, not embedded in it.
A sprinkled pattern is a random and uneven distribution of small, separate elements.
A sprinkled texture is a fine, particulate arrangement scattered sparsely over a background.
A sprinkled surface is characterized by a sparse and random scattering of tiny dots or specks.
A sprinkled object looks like it has been lightly dusted with a powder or small items.
A sprinkled food item has a light and random covering of many small, individual items.
A sprinkled pattern consists of many small, separate specks without a clear order.
A sprinkled surface has been lightly showered with tiny, discrete particles.
stained: A stained surface has an unwanted patch of color that has soaked into the material.
A stained cloth is characterized by discoloration with irregular, poorly defined, or 'bleeding' edges.
Something that is stained visually resembles a wine spill on a tablecloth or a water mark on wood.
On a stained object, the color appears to be absorbed into the material, not just sitting on the surface.
A stained area is a blotchy, uneven patch of different color, often with a darker 'tide mark' at the edge.
A stained mark is a permanent or semi-permanent blemish that alters the base color.
A stained texture consists of irregular, washed-out patches of color.
A stained surface is not uniformly colored due to accidental absorption.
A stained pattern has an accidental and irregular coloration with soft, fuzzy borders.
A stained area is a discolored patch that indicates a spill or contamination.
stratified: A stratified rock is composed of multiple distinct, parallel layers stacked on top of each other.
A stratified object visually resembles sedimentary rock, plywood, or a layered cake.
A stratified structure is characterized by its horizontal or layered composition, viewed in cross-section.
In a stratified material, each layer (stratum) can have different colors or textures.
A stratified object has a visually banded appearance caused by its layered construction.
A stratified structure is built up from successive deposits or sheets of material.
A cross-section of a stratified object reveals a series of parallel bands or layers.
A stratified texture is defined by its stacked, sheet-like components.
A stratified formation consists of distinct, sequential, and parallel layers.
A stratified object is a composition of clearly separated, stacked planes or sheets.
striped: A striped pattern consists of parallel lines or bands of alternating colors.
A striped object has bands that are typically wider and more distinct than in a lined pattern.
Something that is striped visually resembles a zebra, a barcode, or a candy cane.
A striped fabric has a repeating pattern of parallel stripes of uniform or varying widths.
A striped design is a strong, linear, and directional pattern created by adjacent color bands.
A striped surface is decorated with parallel bars of color.
A striped object is a composition of adjacent, elongated bands of contrasting color.
A striped material is characterized by its pattern of parallel, colored strips.
A striped shirt is a classic pattern of alternating colored bands running in one direction.
A striped surface is divided into parallel sections of different colors.
studded: A studded object is a surface decorated with small, raised objects (studs), typically made of metal.
A studded jacket visually resembles a leather jacket with metal studs or a studded tire.
A studded pattern consists of regularly or irregularly spaced bumps or bosses that are added elements.
On a studded object, the studs are discrete, protruding elements on a base surface.
A studded texture is both tactile and visually punctuated by raised points.
A studded belt is a surface adorned with arrays of projecting knobs or nailheads.
A studded material is characterized by its pattern of small, separate, raised points.
A studded texture is a bumpy surface where the bumps are distinct, added, and often uniform elements.
A studded object is a composition of a background surface with raised, separate points attached to it.
A studded surface is ornamented with a pattern of projecting fasteners or knobs.
swirly: A swirly pattern consists of curving, coiling, and looping lines in a fluid motion.
A swirly design visually resembles stirred paint, marbled paper, or a Van Gogh sky.
The swirly texture is characterized by its fluid, rotational, and often non-repeating motion.
A swirly pattern is a chaotic and dynamic design of continuous curves and whorls.
A swirly texture consists of free-form, eddying shapes and convoluted, flowing lines.
A swirly object has a complex, irregular pattern of whorls and curls that suggests movement.
A swirly pattern is less regular and more chaotic than a spiral, with multiple centers of rotation.
The swirly design is a visual representation of turbulence or flowing movement.
A swirly pattern consists of mixed, curling shapes that twist and turn.
A swirly surface is marked with flowing, S-shaped curves and loops.
veined: A veined leaf has a pattern of thin, branching lines spreading across its surface.
A veined object visually resembles veins in a leaf, marble, or on the back of a hand.
A veined surface has a network of interconnected, often irregular, linear channels.
A veined pattern is characterized by its dendritic (tree-like) branching design.
A veined texture consists of fine, meandering lines that split and rejoin.
A veined object has a delicate, web-like pattern of lines that suggests a circulatory system.
In a veined material, the lines often appear as a network for transporting fluids.
A veined surface has an intricate network of fine cracks or threads that branch out.
A veined structure is a linear, branching network embedded in or on a surface.
A veined pattern is reminiscent of a natural vascular system, with main lines splitting into smaller ones.
waffled: A waffled texture is a regular grid of deep, square or diamond-shaped depressions.
A waffled surface visually resembles a breakfast waffle or a waffle-weave towel.
A waffled object has a grid of recessed pockets separated by raised ridges.
A waffled pattern is a three-dimensional, deeply indented grid design.
A waffled texture is characterized by its square-based, pitted appearance in a regular array.
A waffled surface has a regular pattern of sunken squares.
A waffled object has a strong, geometric texture with significant depth and sharp angles.
A waffled design is a crisscross pattern of deep grooves, creating a grid of recessed squares.
A waffled surface has a repeating pattern of uniform, square-shaped indentations.
A waffled structure is like a honeycomb, but with squares or diamonds instead of hexagons.
woven: A woven texture is created by interlacing two sets of threads (warp and weft) at right angles.
A woven object visually resembles a fabric, a basket, or a tapestry.
A woven pattern is a regular design of over-and-under elements, creating a flat, grid-like sheet.
A woven material is characterized by its grid-like, interlaced structure.
A woven surface has a visible crisscross pattern of threads or bands.
A woven texture is made from systematically intertwined strands.
A woven object appears to be constructed from perpendicular, interlaced components.
A woven pattern is a flat, grid-based design of interlocking, perpendicular elements.
A woven fabric has a classic textile pattern of right-angled interlacing.
A woven material is a surface of threads passed over and under each other to form a solid fabric.
wrinkled: A wrinkled surface is covered in a multitude of fine, irregular creases and folds.
A wrinkled object visually resembles crumpled paper, aged skin, or fabric that needs ironing.
A wrinkled texture is characterized by its network of shallow, chaotic, and random lines.
A wrinkled pattern consists of fine, random furrows and ridges without a clear arrangement.
The lines on a wrinkled surface are random, often interconnected, and run in many directions.
A wrinkled material is not smooth or flat due to creasing and contraction.
A wrinkled texture consists of small, tight, and irregular folds.
A wrinkled object has a crinkled or crumpled appearance suggesting compression or age.
A wrinkled surface is marked by a dense network of fine, shallow lines.
The wrinkled effect is the visual appearance of a surface that has lost its tautness.
zigzagged: A zigzagged line is a continuous line forming a series of sharp, angular turns in alternating directions.
A zigzagged edge is a jagged, saw-toothed border defined by a uniformly repeated V-shape.
A zigzagged pattern is a rhythmic arrangement of short diagonal lines joined end-to-end.
A zigzagged structure is defined by acute-angled corners that repeatedly and abruptly reverse direction.
A zigzagged path progresses in a distinct back-and-forth motion.
A zigzagged texture is created by a series of connected, non-perpendicular lines.
The zigzagged shape resembles a stylized lightning bolt or a mountain range silhouette.
A zigzagged object can have a pleated or accordion-like fold creating a repeating triangular motif.
A zigzagged form is a regular, sharp oscillation between two parallel boundaries.
A zigzagged pattern is formed by a repeated sequence of oblique, intersecting line segments.
